parsed_review.summary: CP00-602 is a clean descriptor-only closeout pack covering 10 RP19 units (RP19.P07.M05.S17-RP19.P07.M06.S04, risk class A): the M05 Permission And Audit Binding tail (6 rows) and the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (4 rows). Scope, deliverable distribution {test:2,security_audit:1,hermes_evidence:1,claude_review:1,implementation:1,failure_recovery:4}, and micro-title distribution {Permission And Audit Binding:6,Synthetic Fixture Set:4} are consistent across the baseline, closeout-pack-plan.json, registry CP602_REQUIREMENTS, the contract, model.test.js, and the validate script. Descriptor-only/synthetic-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries are asserted in the no-write attestation, the freezeCp602Result wrapper, per-row guards (descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, raw_payload_included:false), and enforced by validators plus 3 dedicated tests. production_ready:false and runtime_ready:false are correctly not overclaimed; the production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification label, consistent with the standing rule not to weaken production_ready gates. Claude is kept read-only (tools Read/Grep/Glob, read_only:true) and is not final approver (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false throughout). Hermes appears only as descriptor evidence-packet metadata (H19), with emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false. CP178+ hardening rejection rules (auth failure, zero-byte, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields) are encoded in forbidden_review_evidence; the auth preflight passed with non-empty valid-JSON stdout. Changed-file scope matches the pack refs and correctly segregates unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room UI track). The not-yet-present post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are expected to be produced downstream of this review in the hardening sequence and are not a defect. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
